title: clinopodium alpinum subsp. pyrenaeum
intro: Clinopodium alpinum subsp. pyrenaeum, also known as Pyrenean Clinopodium or Pyrenean Wild Basil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Pyrenees. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Clinopodium alpinum subsp. pyrenaeum include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its herbal benefits, and is also used in some traditional culinary dishes, particularly in the cuisine of the Pyrenean region.
